content:
Users often are "missing" emails because they are not familiar with the multiple ways to display messages in Thunderbird. You can read more about appearance, layout, message sorting and threading, ... etc in this help article https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/appearance-panel-layout-message-list-card-view-tab#w_card-view

How many email accounts have you set up in Thunderbird? If you have several accounts the Inbox in "Recent folders" is not necessarily the same Inbox which you compare with in "All Folders". Which are your settings when you open the View menu and click on "Sort By"? (see image)

content:
I also recently created a new outlook.com email account using the settings below. I believe that your settings are incorrect. According to this page from the Microsoft site, the authentication setting must be OAUTH2. That said, I am able to receive email but not send any. I get an "Unable to authenticate to Outgoing server" error message. I've given up on the matter for now, but you should try these "official" Microsoft settings and see if they work for you.

https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/pop-imap-and-smtp-settings-for-outlook-com-d088b986-291d-42b8-9564-9c414e2aa040

If you want to add your Outlook.com account to another email program that supports POP or IMAP, here are the manual server settings you'll need.

If you want to use POP or IMAP to access your email in Outlook.com, you'll first need to enable access.

- Select Settings > Mail > Forwarding and IMAP.

- Under POP and IMAP, toggle the slider for Let devices and apps use POP or Let devices and apps use IMAP to ON depending on the account you are enabling.

- Select Save.


Username: Your email address Password: Your Microsoft account password. (If your password is not being recognized, or if you want to add your Outlook.com account to a smart device like a home security camera, you may need an app password. Learn how to add your Outlook.com account to another mail app or smart device.)

IMAP server: outlook.office365.com IMAP port: 993 IMAP encryption: SSL/TLS Authentication Method: OAuth2/Modern Auth

POP server name: outlook.office365.com POP port: 995 POP encryption: SSL/TLS Authentication Method: OAuth2/Modern Auth

SMTP server name: smtp-mail.outlook.com SMTP port: 587 SMTP encryption: STARTTLS Authentication Method: OAuth2/Modern Auth

content:
Are you saying it's all working for you now? Yay!

My IMAP settings were the same as what you show. I had a different SMTP server name, and I changed it (I assume you made a type and there's an "i" in "offce365") but it still doesn't work. I get the same authentication error. Fortunately, this whole account is just an experiment and I don't depend on it for anything, which gives me time to puzzle it out.

I did find two interesting things, though. One is that in order to get presented with a login password box, I have to delete the password in Saved Passwords for outlook.com AND I have to delete the cookies for both microsoftonline.com AND live.com.

The other is that when they insist on sending me a verification code and I have it sent to one of my other email address, Thunderbird won't check for new mail while the verification box is still up. Meaning, I can't get the code during the current Thunderbird session. I used my phone to check email and got the code that way. When I then entered it in the Thunderbird-presented entry box, THEN the code emails were delivered. Annoying.
creator: rednoise

content:
I trust you always obtain the permissions from people when using their email addresses for something other than sending an email to that person. Data protection and all that kind of stuff. I'm adding that info because some people may not be aware of it.

The format: display name <email address> is the correct format if sending an email to that person when including their provided display name. In Thunderbird, if you want to send an email, you will discover the From will display your name and email address in the same format. When you need to enter someone into the TO or Cc field, the data you have have in the Address book is auto applied. You can locate them by either starting to type the name and options are offered for selection OR you can use the Contacts Sidebar - select address book , select name and click on 'Add to To'

If you receive an email and want to copy the eg: 'From' email address

  • Select email to read
  • In the Message header area locate 'From'
  • Right click on the email address and select 'Copy email address'
  • Paste will then paste just the email address eg: no-reply@support.mozilla.org
  • If you Right click on the email address and select 'Copy name and email address'
  • Paste will then paste the display name and email address in correct format for using in a TO field eg: Mozilla Support Forum <no-reply@support.mozilla.org>

If you receive an email and want to save their email address in a Thunderbird address book: In Message header of email, click on the FRom email address to see a drop down option and select 'Add to Address Book'. It will auto get added to the 'Personal Address Book'

If you wish to alter this and edit other details regarding the contact: In Message header of email,

  • click on the FRom email address to see a drop down option
  • select 'Edit Contact'

You can then choose a different address book and click on 'Done' BUT if you want to add other information click on the button 'Edit Details'. This opens the contact details in the Address Book, thus allowing you to input other data such as 'First name' and 'Last name', telephone numbers, address etc. You will discover the 'display name' the sender included with email address is now in the 'Display name' field and the email address has been added to the 'Email Addresses' field. That email address will not have the format used when sending an email with a display name.

If you want to copy an email address from a contact already saved in an address book: (Please note, this is not necessary if wanting to send them an email as the process is semi automated when you click on Write/New Message)

  • Open Address Book
  • Select name of address book
  • select contact
  • click on 'Edit'
  • Locate 'Email Addresses'
  • highlight the email address
  • right click on highlighted email address
  • select 'Copy'
